mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows INDPRO available as a monthly real activity measure
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward triggered observations, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises sharply, expected household income and aggregate demand weaken, causing investors to mark down forward earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows UNRATE available as a low-revision monthly labor slack measure
failure_condition: Reject if upward UNRATE crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if triggered mean returns are not below non-triggered mean returns with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over several months tightens discount rates and credit conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses upward by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows FEDFUNDS available as a monthly monetary stance measure
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns after FEDFUNDS upward crossings are not lower than unconditional returns in walk-forward tests, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is positive and housing starts are not declining, broad household demand is expanding across goods and construction channels, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and HOUST 3-month change is nonnegative
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes monthly retail sales and housing starts as demand proxies
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST condition does not produce higher 60-day forward returns than either single-series condition alone, or if triggered positive-return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI growth accelerates while the federal funds rate is not rising, investors infer that inflation pressure is eroding real margins and future policy tightening risk is increasing, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS as low-revision monetary and inflation-condition proxies
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration combined with non-rising FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 52% of triggered observations, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals expanding labor income without rising slack, increasing expected consumption and earnings, and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes payroll employment and unemployment rate as monthly labor indicators
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up and unemployment-down signal has lower 20-day average S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns, or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand and tighter household balance-sheet conditions, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses downward by more than 10 percent over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows HOUST available as a monthly housing-demand proxy
failure_condition: Reject if downward HOUST crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations, or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth raises expected aggregate sales and profits, causing equities to reprice upward; positive changes in GDPC1 therefore predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after release availability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses upward relative to the prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes GDPC1 as a quarterly real output measure with high revision risk
failure_condition: Reject if upward GDPC1 growth crossings are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 52% of triggered instances, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for real-time data revisions.
